content: This investigation into VedaCorp Financial Fraud (FIR 1047/2023) reveals a complex, multi-layered scheme to inflate company valuation for IPO purposes. Key findings: 1. ₹12.7 crore in fabricated revenue through 9 shell companies, 2 of which (Sahara Ventures, Nirvana Tech) are central. 2. Digital forgery of board minutes and independent director’s signature. 3. Bribery of auditor Dr. Anjali Kulkarni via ₹15 lakh transfer from Sahara Ventures. 4. Identity theft of Dr. Kulkarni’s Aadhaar to register Sahara Ventures. 5. ₹5 crore transfer to Sahara Ventures was not a client repayment — no such client exists. 6. Whistleblower Rohan Verma’s complaint was genuine; his device was hacked to frame him. 7. Arjun Mehta was physically present at office during forgery of minutes. 8. Priya Mehta accessed internal systems and is registered director of Sahara Ventures despite claims of ignorance. 9. Rajiv Desai coordinated forgery, altered documents, and bribed witnesses. 10. Dr. Kulkarni received bribe, retracted testimony under duress, and her Aadhaar was stolen. Conclusion: Arjun Mehta is the mastermind. Priya Mehta, Rajiv Desai, and Dr. Kulkarni are active accomplices. Evidence sufficient to file charge sheet under IPC 420, 467, 468, 471, 120B, and Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002.
